file_title: AI-Integrated ITSM for Enhanced Healthcare Support
introduction: This presentation explores the potential of integrating AI into our IT Service Management to improve remote care and mobile health app support. We'll discuss the benefits, challenges, and a roadmap for implementation.

subsections.heading: Current ITSM Functionality and Limitations
subsections.content:
• In 2025, our IT team was successful in resolving 57% of incidents related to remote care within a two-hour framework.
• Despite this success, there remains a 43% gap where resolution times exceeded the two-hour mark, indicating room for improvement in our ITSM processes.
• Our current ITSM approach has been beneficial in supporting our healthcare delivery, yet it can be further optimized to handle high-priority incidents more efficiently.
• Utilizing AI-integrated ITSM could potentially reduce the number of unresolved incidents, leading to faster resolution times and improved service delivery.
• Implementing Knowledge Centered Services (KCS) as part of our ITSM could reduce incoming tickets, allowing our team to focus more on complex problem solving.
• Leveraging automated evidence collection and audit preparation, as suggested by TOPdesk, could streamline our compliance with healthcare regulations, saving significant time and resources.
• Enhancing ITSM functionality is crucial to maintain our commitment to delivering seamless experiences to our end-users, both patients and healthcare professionals.
subsections.justification: This content showcases our current ITSM performance and areas for improvement, answering the DR Question by demonstrating the potential of AI-integrated ITSM in enhancing service delivery and regulatory compliance.

subsections.heading: Potential of AI Integration in ITSM
subsections.content:
• AI has the potential to automate routine queries, freeing up IT personnel for complex issues
• AI can predict and prevent potential IT disruptions using predictive analytics
• AI can enhance multi-channel engagement by providing instant responses across various platforms
• AI can streamline the ITSM process by integrating with other business software
• An AI-powered ITSM platform can potentially improve first-time resolution rates and reduce ticket volume
• AI has the potential to personalize IT services based on user behavior and preferences
subsections.justification: Focuses on general benefits of AI in ITSM but doesn't address its use in supporting remote care and mobile health apps.

subsections.heading: Roadmap for AI-Integrated ITSM Implementation
subsections.content:
• Phase 1: Conduct a thorough audit of the current ITSM platform (Q2 2025)
• Phase 2: Identify potential AI solutions and evaluate their compatibility with our current systems (Q3 2025)
• Phase 3: Pilot AI solutions in a controlled environment (Q4 2025)
• Phase 4: Analyze pilot results and make necessary adjustments (Q1 2026)
• Phase 5: Roll out AI-integrated ITSM platform company-wide (Q2 2026)
• Phase 6: Regularly monitor and update AI solutions for continuous improvement (Q3 2026 onwards)
subsections.justification: Details the implementation plan for AI in ITSM, but doesn't mention its impact on remote care or mobile health apps.

conclusion: With careful planning and execution, AI-integrated ITSM has the potential to significantly enhance our support mechanisms for remote care and mobile health apps. Moving forward, we'll need to focus on the development of a comprehensive implementation plan that accounts for regulatory compliance and customer experience.
